mnemonics.title: TYPES OF LOCKS
mnemonics.content: SHARED LOCKS CAN READ TOGETHER, EXCLUSIVE LOCKS CANNOT BREATHE (BE READ OR WRITTEN BY OTHERS)

mnemonics.title: FILE SHARING ISSUES
mnemonics.content: RACE (Race conditions, Access control, Consistency, Enforcement mechanisms)

mnemonics.title: READERS-WRITERS
mnemonics.content: MANY READERS MAY, ONE WRITER MUST WAIT - READERS DON'T BLOCK EACH OTHER, WRITER BLOCKS EVERYONE

acronyms.expansion: Share Mode Flag - enables file descriptor to participate in file sharing rules with other processes
acronyms.usage: Used in open() system call to enable concurrent file access with other processes that also open the file with O_SHRW

acronyms.expansion: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability - properties of transactional file access
acronyms.usage: Ensures file operations complete fully, maintain consistency, are isolated from other operations, and persist after completion

acronyms.expansion: File Control - UNIX system call for file locking and other descriptor operations
acronyms.usage: Used with F_SETLK/F_SETLKW to acquire/release file locks in UNIX systems

visualTips.visualization: Think of a reading room (shared lock) where multiple people can read together, but a writing room (exclusive lock) where only one person can enter at a time
Imagine two people simultaneously trying to update the same document on a shared drive - whichever saves last overwrites the other's changes
Think of a bank transfer as a single indivisible operation - either the money moves completely or it doesn't, no partial state
Imagine a library book with a time limit - if not renewed within the deadline, it automatically becomes available to others

analogies.analogy: Like a restroom sign - shared lock is like a 'Vacant' sign that many can see, exclusive lock is like a 'Occupied' sign that prevents anyone else from entering
Like working on a personal copy of a document - your changes only affect your version until you save and others reopen the file
Like multiple people having copies of a meeting agenda - if someone updates their copy, others still have outdated information until they get the new version
Mandatory is like a locked door that physically cannot be opened without the key; Advisory is like a 'Do Not Disturb' sign that others can choose to ignore
